serverInfo.name: mcpval
serverInfo.title: MCP Validator Local MCP
serverInfo.description: Local MCP server that wraps the mcpval CLI so MCP clients can validate remote and local servers through MCP tools.

payload.instructions: Use health_check first for a quick bootstrap and protocol check. Use discover for remote HTTP MCP endpoints when you need a capability catalog. Use validate for full compliance, security, and AI-safety analysis, especially for local or stdio targets. Provide server as either an absolute MCP endpoint URL or a stdio command.

serverInfo.name: mcpval
serverInfo.title: MCP Validator Local MCP
serverInfo.description: Local MCP server that wraps the mcpval CLI so MCP clients can validate remote and local servers through MCP tools.

payload.instructions: Use health_check first for a quick bootstrap and protocol check. Use discover for remote HTTP MCP endpoints when you need a capability catalog. Use validate for full compliance, security, and AI-safety analysis, especially for local or stdio targets. Provide server as either an absolute MCP endpoint URL or a stdio command.

probes.requirement: STDIO messages must be individual UTF-8 JSON-RPC messages delimited by newlines and must not contain embedded literal newlines.
probes.expected: One stdout line containing one JSON-RPC message and no embedded newline characters.
probes.actual: STDIO status 200; stdout line length 57; stderr captured

probes.requirement: STDIO servers must write only valid MCP JSON-RPC messages to stdout.
probes.expected: Every observed stdout line is a valid JSON-RPC object with jsonrpc=2.0 and a request, notification, response, or error shape.
probes.actual: STDIO status 200; stdout line length 57; stderr captured

probes.requirement: STDIO servers may write UTF-8 log strings to stderr, and clients should not treat stderr output as an MCP failure by itself.
probes.expected: stderr is captured as log evidence and is not parsed as MCP stdout.
probes.actual: stderr log output was captured and preserved as non-failure evidence.

probes.requirement: Malformed raw STDIO probes that are dropped by the SDK parser boundary must be reported as inconclusive/skipped, not as server protocol failures.
probes.expected: Raw malformed parser-boundary cases are separated from observable JSON-RPC error-response evidence.
probes.actual: No raw STDIO parser-boundary drops were observed.

probes.requirement: STDIO clients should close stdin, terminate the subprocess when needed, and leave no running child process after shutdown.
probes.expected: The process exits during shutdown cleanup and the adapter can restart it for subsequent probes.

protocolCompliance.message: declared capabilities: logging, prompts, prompts.listChanged, resources, resources.listChanged, tools, tools.listChanged | roots/list: not advertised | logging/setLevel: supported | sampling/createMessage: not advertised | completion/complete: not advertised

findings.summary: Batch processing probe skipped because the active embedded schema context does not advertise batch JSON-RPC envelopes.
findings.recommendation: Do not treat raw JSON-RPC batch arrays as a hard failure when the negotiated transport does not advertise reliable batch support.

findings.summary: Server responded to logging/setLevel, indicating optional logging controls are available.
findings.recommendation: Keep capability advertisement and method support aligned so clients can rely on initialize without speculative probing.

triggeredDecisions.summary: MAY requirement failed: MAY: Server supports sampling/createMessage
MAY requirement failed: MAY: Server supports roots/list
MAY requirement failed: MAY: Server supports completion/complete

testScenarios.expectedBehavior: STDIO transport should NOT use HTTP authentication headers
testScenarios.actualBehavior: ✅ Correctly using STDIO transport (no HTTP auth)
testScenarios.complianceReason: MCP Spec: STDIO implementations should NOT follow HTTP authorization spec

testScenarios.expectedBehavior: Environment variables may carry credentials for STDIO servers
testScenarios.complianceReason: Credentials appear to be passed via environment variables

findings.summary: Synthetic load probe executed against tools/list using 20 requests at concurrency 4.
findings.recommendation: Interpret this as a generic pressure probe, not a workload-specific SLA benchmark.

errorScenarioResults.actualResponse: HTTP 400; JSON-RPC error -32601: Method not found

errorScenarioResults.expectedResponse: Validator-induced timeout should surface a transport failure and the endpoint should respond to a follow-up recovery probe.
errorScenarioResults.actualResponse: Validator induced a zero-window STDIO read timeout and then drained the pending response.

errorScenarioResults.expectedResponse: Validator-induced transport interruption should not leave the endpoint unresponsive to a follow-up recovery probe.
errorScenarioResults.actualResponse: Validator terminated the STDIO child process and restarted it for recovery verification.
